text:
There was a quaint little library tucked into an old brick building in the town square. It had been here since before anybody could remember. People would come just to stand in the cozy little library, feel the dust on the shelves, and let their minds wander. Inside, there was a special room. It was tucked between two towering bookcases that housed ancient tomes. It was filled with clocks and old mirrors, a sort of mirror maze filled with their reflections.

In this peculiar room lived a lonely old cat named Mittens. Mittens didn't normally meow; she communicated through tail positions: fluffy paws meant she's relaxed, while sleek fingers meant she's upset. But today, Mittens was agitated.

There was a mysterious silence in the library that day, different. People were unusually quiet. The only sounds were the ticking of the clocks and Mittens scratching at the window. She watched a spider weaving across the mirror-paneled walls. It was a mesmerizing dance.

Suddenly
